summary: Verified the sitemap.xml / robots.txt SEO fix on the Next.js marketing site (http://localhost:3100). Static files now serve correctly with all 10 URLs, robots.txt contains required directives, no conflicting dynamic routes remain, and the homepage renders with Finnish hero copy + navbar. All 3 acceptance criteria PASS.
critical_code_review_comments: Static sitemap.xml requires manual sync if new public routes are added — consider documenting this in README or restoring a build-time sitemap generator (not Next.js dynamic route) to avoid drift.
robots.txt currently allows '/admin/login' subroute discovery only via Disallow on '/admin' and '/admin/' — acceptable, but consider adding 'Disallow: /api/' if any internal API paths exist.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Next.js marketing site runs on http://localhost:3100 (start via `cd /app/web && PORT=3100 yarn start` if not running). SEO files are now static at /app/web/public/{sitemap.xml,robots.txt}. The old dynamic routes src/app/sitemap.ts and src/app/robots.ts have been removed and are NOT present in .next/app-path-routes-manifest.json. Do not test the Expo app for SEO-file tasks.
rca_of_issue: Original bug: empty sitemap. RCA: Next.js dynamic metadata routes (app/sitemap.ts & app/robots.ts) only render at runtime via the Node server; on static/misrouted hosting they returned empty. Fix verified: dynamic .ts files removed and replaced with real static files in /public, which are served directly by the web server regardless of rendering mode. Curl confirms 200 + correct Content-Type + full content on all 3 endpoints.
